search_text: Dynamic Median Consensus for Marine Multi-Robot Systems Using Acoustic Communication 使用声学通信的海洋多机器人系统的动态中值共识 In this paper, we present a dynamic median consensus protocol for multi-agent systems using acoustic communication. The motivating target scenario is a multi-agent system consisting of underwater robots acting as intelligent sensors, applied to continuous monitoring of the state of a marine environment. The proposed protocol allows each agent to track the median value of individual measurements of all agents through local communication with neighbouring agents. Median is chosen as a measure robust to outliers, as opposed to average value, which is usually used. In contrast to the existing consensus protocols, the proposed protocol is dynamic, uses a switching communication topology and converges to median of measured signals. Stability and correctness of the protocol are theoretically proven. The protocol is tested in simulation, and accuracy and influence of protocol parameters on the system output are analyzed. The protocol is implemented and validated by a set of experiments on an underwater group of robots comprising of aMussel units. This experimental setup is one of the first deployments of any type of consensus protocol for an underwater setting. Both simulation and experimental results confirm the correctness of the presented approach. 在本文中，我们提出了一种使用声学通信的多智能体系统的动态中值共识协议。激励目标场景是由水下机器人作为智能传感器组成的多智能体系统，应用于连续监测海洋环境的状态。所提出的协议允许每个代理通过与相邻代理的本地通信来跟踪所有代理的单独测量的中值。选择中位数作为对异常值稳健的度量，而不是通常使用的平均值。与现有的共识协议相比，所提出的协议是动态的，使用交换通信拓扑并收敛到测量信号的中值。理论上证明了协议的稳定性和正确性。对协议进行了仿真测试，分析了协议参数的准确性以及对系统输出的影响。该协议是通过对由 aMussel 单元组成的水下机器人组进行的一组实验来实现和验证的。该实验设置是水下环境中任何类型共识协议的首批部署之一。仿真和实验结果都证实了所提出方法的正确性。 本文提出一种基于声学通信的动态中位数共识协议，用于水下多机器人系统，通过局部通信实现对环境状态测量值的鲁棒中位数跟踪。 Goran Vasiljevic,Tamara Petrovic,Barbara Arbanas,Stjepan Bogdan cs.RO,eess.SY

authors_text: Rakshit S. Kothari,Reynold J. Bailey,Christopher Kanan,Jeff B. Pelz,Gabriel J. Diaz
title: EllSeg-Gen, towards Domain Generalization for head-mounted eyetracking
title_zh: EllSeg-Gen，致力于头戴式眼球追踪的领域泛化
abstract: The study of human gaze behavior in natural contexts requires algorithms for gaze estimation that are robust to a wide range of imaging conditions. However, algorithms often fail to identify features such as the iris and pupil centroid in the presence of reflective artifacts and occlusions. Previous work has shown that convolutional networks excel at extracting gaze features despite the presence of such artifacts. However, these networks often perform poorly on data unseen during training. This work follows the intuition that jointly training a convolutional network with multiple datasets learns a generalized representation of eye parts. We compare the performance of a single model trained with multiple datasets against a pool of models trained on individual datasets. Results indicate that models tested on datasets in which eye images exhibit higher appearance variability benefit from multiset training. In contrast, dataset-specific models generalize better onto eye images with lower appearance variability.
summary: The study of human gaze behavior in natural contexts requires algorithms for gaze estimation that are robust to a wide range of imaging conditions. However, algorithms often fail to identify features such as the iris and pupil centroid in the presence of reflective artifacts and occlusions. Previous work has shown that convolutional networks excel at extracting gaze features despite the presence of such artifacts. However, these networks often perform poorly on data unseen during training. This work follows the intuition that jointly training a convolutional network with multiple datasets learns a generalized representation of eye parts. We compare the performance of a single model trained with multiple datasets against a pool of models trained on individual datasets. Results indicate that models tested on datasets in which eye images exhibit higher appearance variability benefit from multiset training. In contrast, dataset-specific models generalize better onto eye images with lower appearance variability.
abstract_zh: 对自然环境中人类注视行为的研究需要对各种成像条件都具有鲁棒性的注视估计算法。然而，在存在反射伪影和遮挡的情况下，算法通常无法识别虹膜和瞳孔质心等特征。之前的工作表明，尽管存在此类伪影，但卷积网络仍擅长提取注视特征。然而，这些网络通常在训练期间未见的数据上表现不佳。这项工作遵循这样的直觉：使用多个数据集联合训练卷积网络可以学习眼睛部位的广义表示。我们将使用多个数据集训练的单个模型与在单个数据集上训练的模型池的性能进行比较。结果表明，在眼睛图像表现出较高外观变异性的数据集上测试的模型受益于多集训练。相比之下，特定于数据集的模型可以更好地推广到具有较低外观变异性的眼睛图像。

AI.tldr: 本文提出EllSeg-Gen方法，通过多数据集联合训练提升头戴式眼动追踪算法在不同光照和遮挡条件下的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有眼动追踪算法在面对反光、遮挡等复杂场景时易失效，且单数据集训练的模型泛化能力不足，需探索跨域通用的特征表示方法。
AI.method: 对比单模型多数据集联合训练与多模型单数据集训练的效果，分析不同数据集外观变化性对模型泛化能力的影响。
AI.result: 多数据集训练的模型在外观变化性高的数据集上表现更优，而单数据集训练的模型在低变化性场景中更具优势。
AI.conclusion: 联合多数据集训练能有效提升模型对复杂场景的适应性，但需根据数据集外观变化特性选择训练策略。

authors_text: Jie Ren,Mingjie Li,Meng Zhou,Shih-Han Chan,Quanshi Zhang
title: Towards Theoretical Analysis of Transformation Complexity of ReLU DNNs
title_zh: ReLU DNN 转换复杂性的理论分析
abstract: This paper aims to theoretically analyze the complexity of feature transformations encoded in piecewise linear DNNs with ReLU layers. We propose metrics to measure three types of complexities of transformations based on the information theory. We further discover and prove the strong correlation between the complexity and the disentanglement of transformations. Based on the proposed metrics, we analyze two typical phenomena of the change of the transformation complexity during the training process, and explore the ceiling of a DNN's complexity. The proposed metrics can also be used as a loss to learn a DNN with the minimum complexity, which also controls the over-fitting level of the DNN and influences adversarial robustness, adversarial transferability, and knowledge consistency. Comprehensive comparative studies have provided new perspectives to understand the DNN.
summary: This paper aims to theoretically analyze the complexity of feature transformations encoded in piecewise linear DNNs with ReLU layers. We propose metrics to measure three types of complexities of transformations based on the information theory. We further discover and prove the strong correlation between the complexity and the disentanglement of transformations. Based on the proposed metrics, we analyze two typical phenomena of the change of the transformation complexity during the training process, and explore the ceiling of a DNN's complexity. The proposed metrics can also be used as a loss to learn a DNN with the minimum complexity, which also controls the over-fitting level of the DNN and influences adversarial robustness, adversarial transferability, and knowledge consistency. Comprehensive comparative studies have provided new perspectives to understand the DNN.
abstract_zh: 本文旨在从理论上分析具有 ReLU 层的分段线性 DNN 中编码的特征变换的复杂性。我们提出了基于信息论的度量来衡量三种类型的转换复杂性。我们进一步发现并证明了变换的复杂性和解开性之间的强相关性。基于所提出的指标，我们分析了训练过程中变换复杂度变化的两种典型现象，并探索了 DNN 复杂度的上限。所提出的指标还可以用作学习具有最小复杂度的 DNN 的损失，这也控制了 DNN 的过拟合水平并影响对抗性鲁棒性、对抗性可迁移性和知识一致性。全面的比较研究为理解 DNN 提供了新的视角。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在从理论上分析ReLU神经网络的转换复杂度，提出基于信息论的度量指标，研究复杂度与解缠关系，并探讨其在训练过程中的变化及上限。
AI.motivation: 现有研究缺乏对ReLU DNN特征转换复杂度的系统性理论分析，而复杂度直接影响模型性能、过拟合控制及对抗鲁棒性。
AI.method: 基于信息论提出三种复杂度度量指标，证明其与解缠的强相关性，分析训练中复杂度变化现象，并将度量作为损失函数优化网络。
AI.result: 发现复杂度与解缠的强关联，揭示训练中复杂度变化的两种典型现象，确定DNN复杂度上限，验证度量在控制过拟合和提升鲁棒性中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 提出的复杂度度量为理解DNN提供了新视角，可指导模型设计，平衡性能与鲁棒性，并影响对抗样本生成与知识迁移。

motivation: 现有研究缺乏对ReLU DNN特征转换复杂度的系统性理论分析，而复杂度直接影响模型性能、过拟合控制及对抗鲁棒性。
method: 基于信息论提出三种复杂度度量指标，证明其与解缠的强相关性，分析训练中复杂度变化现象，并将度量作为损失函数优化网络。
result: 发现复杂度与解缠的强关联，揭示训练中复杂度变化的两种典型现象，确定DNN复杂度上限，验证度量在控制过拟合和提升鲁棒性中的有效性。
conclusion: 提出的复杂度度量为理解DNN提供了新视角，可指导模型设计，平衡性能与鲁棒性，并影响对抗样本生成与知识迁移。
search_text: Towards Theoretical Analysis of Transformation Complexity of ReLU DNNs ReLU DNN 转换复杂性的理论分析 This paper aims to theoretically analyze the complexity of feature transformations encoded in piecewise linear DNNs with ReLU layers. We propose metrics to measure three types of complexities of transformations based on the information theory. We further discover and prove the strong correlation between the complexity and the disentanglement of transformations. Based on the proposed metrics, we analyze two typical phenomena of the change of the transformation complexity during the training process, and explore the ceiling of a DNN's complexity. The proposed metrics can also be used as a loss to learn a DNN with the minimum complexity, which also controls the over-fitting level of the DNN and influences adversarial robustness, adversarial transferability, and knowledge consistency. Comprehensive comparative studies have provided new perspectives to understand the DNN. 本文旨在从理论上分析具有 ReLU 层的分段线性 DNN 中编码的特征变换的复杂性。我们提出了基于信息论的度量来衡量三种类型的转换复杂性。我们进一步发现并证明了变换的复杂性和解开性之间的强相关性。基于所提出的指标，我们分析了训练过程中变换复杂度变化的两种典型现象，并探索了 DNN 复杂度的上限。所提出的指标还可以用作学习具有最小复杂度的 DNN 的损失，这也控制了 DNN 的过拟合水平并影响对抗性鲁棒性、对抗性可迁移性和知识一致性。全面的比较研究为理解 DNN 提供了新的视角。 本文旨在从理论上分析ReLU神经网络的转换复杂度，提出基于信息论的度量指标，研究复杂度与解缠关系，并探讨其在训练过程中的变化及上限。 Jie Ren,Mingjie Li,Meng Zhou,Shih-Han Chan,Quanshi Zhang cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Clémentin Boittiaux,Ricard Marxer,Claire Dune,Aurélien Arnaubec,Vincent Hugel
title: Homography-Based Loss Function for Camera Pose Regression
title_zh: 用于相机姿态回归的基于单应性的损失函数
comment: ef:IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters 7 (3), pp.6242-6249 (2022)
comments: ef:IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters 7 (3), pp.6242-6249 (2022)
abstract: Some recent visual-based relocalization algorithms rely on deep learning methods to perform camera pose regression from image data. This paper focuses on the loss functions that embed the error between two poses to perform deep learning based camera pose regression. Existing loss functions are either difficult-to-tune multi-objective functions or present unstable reprojection errors that rely on ground truth 3D scene points and require a two-step training. To deal with these issues, we introduce a novel loss function which is based on a multiplane homography integration. This new function does not require prior initialization and only depends on physically interpretable hyperparameters. Furthermore, the experiments carried out on well established relocalization datasets show that it minimizes best the mean square reprojection error during training when compared with existing loss functions.
summary: Some recent visual-based relocalization algorithms rely on deep learning methods to perform camera pose regression from image data. This paper focuses on the loss functions that embed the error between two poses to perform deep learning based camera pose regression. Existing loss functions are either difficult-to-tune multi-objective functions or present unstable reprojection errors that rely on ground truth 3D scene points and require a two-step training. To deal with these issues, we introduce a novel loss function which is based on a multiplane homography integration. This new function does not require prior initialization and only depends on physically interpretable hyperparameters. Furthermore, the experiments carried out on well established relocalization datasets show that it minimizes best the mean square reprojection error during training when compared with existing loss functions.
abstract_zh: 最近一些基于视觉的重定位算法依靠深度学习方法从图像数据执行相机姿态回归。本文重点研究嵌入两个姿势之间的误差的损失函数，以执行基于深度学习的相机姿势回归。现有的损失函数要么是难以调整的多目标函数，要么存在不稳定的重投影误差，这些误差依赖于地面实况 3D 场景点并且需要两步训练。为了解决这些问题，我们引入了一种基于多平面单应积分的新型损失函数。这个新函数不需要事先初始化，仅依赖于物理上可解释的超参数。此外，在完善的重定位数据集上进行的实验表明，与现有的损失函数相比，它可以最大程度地减少训练期间的均方重投影误差。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于多平面单应性集成的新型损失函数，用于改进深度学习中的相机位姿回归任务。
AI.motivation: 现有相机位姿回归损失函数存在多目标调参困难、依赖真实3D点且需要两步训练等缺陷，导致重投影误差不稳定。
AI.method: 设计融合多平面单应性约束的损失函数，无需先验初始化且仅依赖物理可解释的超参数进行端到端训练。
AI.result: 在标准重定位数据集上实验表明，该方法在训练过程中能最小化均方重投影误差，优于现有损失函数。
AI.conclusion: 新损失函数通过单应性约束实现了更稳定可靠的相机位姿回归，为视觉重定位任务提供了有效解决方案。

authors_text: Adalberto Claudio Quiros,Nicolas Coudray,Anna Yeaton,Xinyu Yang,Bojing Liu,Hortense Le,Luis Chiriboga,Afreen Karimkhan,Navneet Narula,David A. Moore,Christopher Y. Park,Harvey Pass,Andre L. Moreira,John Le Quesne,Aristotelis Tsirigos,Ke Yuan
title: Mapping the landscape of histomorphological cancer phenotypes using self-supervised learning on unlabeled, unannotated pathology slides
title_zh: 在未标记、未注释的病理学幻灯片上使用自我监督学习来绘制组织形态学癌症表型的图谱
abstract: Definitive cancer diagnosis and management depend upon the extraction of information from microscopy images by pathologists. These images contain complex information requiring time-consuming expert human interpretation that is prone to human bias. Supervised deep learning approaches have proven powerful for classification tasks, but they are inherently limited by the cost and quality of annotations used for training these models. To address this limitation of supervised methods, we developed Histomorphological Phenotype Learning (HPL), a fully blue{self-}supervised methodology that requires no expert labels or annotations and operates via the automatic discovery of discriminatory image features in small image tiles. Tiles are grouped into morphologically similar clusters which constitute a library of histomorphological phenotypes, revealing trajectories from benign to malignant tissue via inflammatory and reactive phenotypes. These clusters have distinct features which can be identified using orthogonal methods, linking histologic, molecular and clinical phenotypes. Applied to lung cancer tissues, we show that they align closely with patient survival, with histopathologically recognised tumor types and growth patterns, and with transcriptomic measures of immunophenotype. We then demonstrate that these properties are maintained in a multi-cancer study. These results show the clusters represent recurrent host responses and modes of tumor growth emerging under natural selection. Code, pre-trained models, learned embeddings, and documentation are available to the community at https://github.com/AdalbertoCq/Histomorphological-Phenotype-Learning
summary: Definitive cancer diagnosis and management depend upon the extraction of information from microscopy images by pathologists. These images contain complex information requiring time-consuming expert human interpretation that is prone to human bias. Supervised deep learning approaches have proven powerful for classification tasks, but they are inherently limited by the cost and quality of annotations used for training these models. To address this limitation of supervised methods, we developed Histomorphological Phenotype Learning (HPL), a fully blue{self-}supervised methodology that requires no expert labels or annotations and operates via the automatic discovery of discriminatory image features in small image tiles. Tiles are grouped into morphologically similar clusters which constitute a library of histomorphological phenotypes, revealing trajectories from benign to malignant tissue via inflammatory and reactive phenotypes. These clusters have distinct features which can be identified using orthogonal methods, linking histologic, molecular and clinical phenotypes. Applied to lung cancer tissues, we show that they align closely with patient survival, with histopathologically recognised tumor types and growth patterns, and with transcriptomic measures of immunophenotype. We then demonstrate that these properties are maintained in a multi-cancer study. These results show the clusters represent recurrent host responses and modes of tumor growth emerging under natural selection. Code, pre-trained models, learned embeddings, and documentation are available to the community at https://github.com/AdalbertoCq/Histomorphological-Phenotype-Learning
abstract_zh: 最终的癌症诊断和治疗取决于病理学家从显微镜图像中提取信息。这些图像包含复杂的信息，需要耗时的专家人工解释，并且容易产生人为偏见。事实证明，有监督的深度学习方法对于分类任务来说非常强大，但它们本质上受到用于训练这些模型的注释的成本和质量的限制。为了解决监督方法的这一局限性，我们开发了组织形态学表型学习（HPL），这是一种完全蓝色的{自}监督方法，不需要专家标签或注释，并通过自动发现小图像块中的歧视性图像特征来进行操作。图块被分为形态相似的簇，构成组织形态表型库，通过炎症和反应表型揭示从良性组织到恶性组织的轨迹。这些簇具有独特的特征，可以使用正交方法进行识别，将组织学、分子和临床表型联系起来。应用于肺癌组织，我们发现它们与患者的生存率、组织病理学上识别的肿瘤类型和生长模式以及免疫表型的转录组学测量密切相关。然后我们证明这些特性在多种癌症研究中得以保留。这些结果表明，这些簇代表了反复出现的宿主反应和自然选择下出现的肿瘤生长模式。社区可以在 https://github.com/AdalbertoCq/Histomorphological-Phenotype-Learning 上获取代码、预训练模型、学习嵌入和文档

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需专家标注的自监督学习方法HPL，通过分析未标注的病理切片图像，自动发现组织形态学特征并构建癌症表型图谱。
AI.motivation: 传统监督学习依赖昂贵且易出错的专家标注，而病理图像分析需要处理复杂且主观的形态学信息，亟需无需标注的自动化方法。
AI.method: 开发Histomorphological Phenotype Learning（HPL）框架，通过小图像块的自监督学习自动聚类形态学相似区域，生成表型簇并关联分子与临床数据。
AI.result: 在肺癌及多癌种研究中，表型簇与患者生存率、肿瘤类型、免疫表型等显著相关，揭示了肿瘤生长模式与宿主反应的自然选择规律。
AI.conclusion: HPL成功实现了无标注病理图像的表型挖掘，为癌症机制研究和精准医疗提供了可复用的计算工具与数据资源。

Moore,Christopher Y. Park,Harvey Pass,Andre L. Moreira,John Le Quesne,Aristotelis Tsirigos,Ke Yuan cs.CV,cs.LG

title: Probabilistic Symmetry for Multi-Agent Dynamics
title_zh: 多智能体动力学的概率对称性
abstract: Learning multi-agent dynamics is a core AI problem with broad applications in robotics and autonomous driving. While most existing works focus on deterministic prediction, producing probabilistic forecasts to quantify uncertainty and assess risks is critical for downstream decision-making tasks such as motion planning and collision avoidance. Multi-agent dynamics often contains internal symmetry. By leveraging symmetry, specifically rotation equivariance, we can improve not only the prediction accuracy but also uncertainty calibration. We introduce Energy Score, a proper scoring rule, to evaluate probabilistic predictions. We propose a novel deep dynamics model, Probabilistic Equivariant Continuous COnvolution (PECCO) for probabilistic prediction of multi-agent trajectories. PECCO extends equivariant continuous convolution to model the joint velocity distribution of multiple agents. It uses dynamics integration to propagate the uncertainty from velocity to position. On both synthetic and real-world datasets, PECCO shows significant improvements in accuracy and calibration compared to non-equivariant baselines.
summary: Learning multi-agent dynamics is a core AI problem with broad applications in robotics and autonomous driving. While most existing works focus on deterministic prediction, producing probabilistic forecasts to quantify uncertainty and assess risks is critical for downstream decision-making tasks such as motion planning and collision avoidance. Multi-agent dynamics often contains internal symmetry. By leveraging symmetry, specifically rotation equivariance, we can improve not only the prediction accuracy but also uncertainty calibration. We introduce Energy Score, a proper scoring rule, to evaluate probabilistic predictions. We propose a novel deep dynamics model, Probabilistic Equivariant Continuous COnvolution (PECCO) for probabilistic prediction of multi-agent trajectories. PECCO extends equivariant continuous convolution to model the joint velocity distribution of multiple agents. It uses dynamics integration to propagate the uncertainty from velocity to position. On both synthetic and real-world datasets, PECCO shows significant improvements in accuracy and calibration compared to non-equivariant baselines.
abstract_zh: 学习多智能体动力学是一个核心人工智能问题，在机器人和自动驾驶领域有着广泛的应用。虽然大多数现有工作侧重于确定性预测，但生成概率预测来量化不确定性和评估风险对于运动规划和避免碰撞等下游决策任务至关重要。多主体动力学通常包含内部对称性。通过利用对称性，特别是旋转等方差，我们不仅可以提高预测精度，还可以提高不确定性校准。我们引入能量分数（Energy Score），一种适当的评分规则，来评估概率预测。我们提出了一种新颖的深度动力学模型，即概率等变连续卷积（PECCO），用于多智能体轨迹的概率预测。 PECCO 扩展了等变连续卷积来模拟多个智能体的联合速度分布。它使用动力学积分将不确定性从速度传播到位置。在合成数据集和真实世界数据集上，与非等变基线相比，PECCO 在准确性和校准方面均显示出显着改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于概率对称性的多智能体动态预测模型PECCO，通过旋转等变性提升预测精度与不确定性校准。
AI.motivation: 现有方法多关注确定性预测，但多智能体动态需量化不确定性以支持决策。利用对称性可提升预测可靠性与风险评估能力。
AI.method: 设计PECCO模型，扩展等变连续卷积以建模多智能体联合速度分布，并通过动力学积分传播不确定性，引入能量得分评估概率预测。
AI.result: 在合成与真实数据集上，PECCO相较非等变基线模型在精度与校准效果上均有显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 结合概率建模与对称性约束的PECCO有效解决了多智能体动态预测中的不确定性量化问题。

title: Zero-Episode Few-Shot Contrastive Predictive Coding: Solving intelligence tests without prior training
title_zh: 零情节少样本对比预测编码：无需事先训练即可解决智力测试
abstract: Video prediction models often combine three components: an encoder from pixel space to a small latent space, a latent space prediction model, and a generative model back to pixel space. However, the large and unpredictable pixel space makes training such models difficult, requiring many training examples. We argue that finding a predictive latent variable and using it to evaluate the consistency of a future image enables data-efficient predictions because it precludes the necessity of a generative model training. To demonstrate it, we created sequence completion intelligence tests in which the task is to identify a predictably changing feature in a sequence of images and use this prediction to select the subsequent image. We show that a one-dimensional Markov Contrastive Predictive Coding (M-CPC_1D) model solves these tests efficiently, with only five examples. Finally, we demonstrate the usefulness of M-CPC_1D in solving two tasks without prior training: anomaly detection and stochastic movement video prediction.
summary: Video prediction models often combine three components: an encoder from pixel space to a small latent space, a latent space prediction model, and a generative model back to pixel space. However, the large and unpredictable pixel space makes training such models difficult, requiring many training examples. We argue that finding a predictive latent variable and using it to evaluate the consistency of a future image enables data-efficient predictions because it precludes the necessity of a generative model training. To demonstrate it, we created sequence completion intelligence tests in which the task is to identify a predictably changing feature in a sequence of images and use this prediction to select the subsequent image. We show that a one-dimensional Markov Contrastive Predictive Coding (M-CPC_1D) model solves these tests efficiently, with only five examples. Finally, we demonstrate the usefulness of M-CPC_1D in solving two tasks without prior training: anomaly detection and stochastic movement video prediction.
abstract_zh: 视频预测模型通常结合三个组件：从像素空间到小型潜在空间的编码器、潜在空间预测模型以及返回像素空间的生成模型。然而，巨大且不可预测的像素空间使得训练此类模型变得困难，需要许多训练样本。我们认为，找到预测潜变量并使用它来评估未来图像的一致性可以实现数据高效的预测，因为它消除了生成模型训练的必要性。为了证明这一点，我们创建了序列完成智能测试，其中的任务是识别图像序列中可预测变化的特征，并使用此预测来选择后续图像。我们证明一维马尔可夫对比预测编码 (M-CPC_1D) 模型仅用五个示例就可以有效地解决这些测试。最后，我们展示了 M-CPC_1D 在无需事先训练的情况下解决两个任务的有用性：异常检测和随机运动视频预测。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需预训练即可高效解决智力测试的对比预测编码模型。
AI.motivation: 传统视频预测模型因像素空间复杂需大量训练数据，而作者认为通过预测潜在变量可避免生成模型训练，实现数据高效预测。
AI.method: 设计一维马尔可夫对比预测编码（M-CPC_1D）模型，通过潜在空间预测未来图像一致性，无需生成模型即可完成序列补全任务。
AI.result: M-CPC_1D模型仅用五个示例即可解决序列补全测试，并成功应用于异常检测和随机运动视频预测任务。
AI.conclusion: 该方法证明了通过预测潜在变量可替代生成模型，为零样本/少样本学习提供了高效解决方案。

authors_text: Jun Yu,Hao Chang,Keda Lu,Liwen Zhang,Shenshen Du,Zhong Zhang
title: Scene Clustering Based Pseudo-labeling Strategy for Multi-modal Aerial View Object Classification
title_zh: 基于场景聚类的多模态鸟瞰目标分类伪标记策略
abstract: Multi-modal aerial view object classification (MAVOC) in Automatic target recognition (ATR), although an important and challenging problem, has been under studied. This paper firstly finds that fine-grained data, class imbalance and various shooting conditions preclude the representational ability of general image classification. Moreover, the MAVOC dataset has scene aggregation characteristics. By exploiting these properties, we propose Scene Clustering Based Pseudo-labeling Strategy (SCP-Label), a simple yet effective method to employ in post-processing. The SCP-Label brings greater accuracy by assigning the same label to objects within the same scene while also mitigating bias and confusion with model ensembles. Its performance surpasses the official baseline by a large margin of +20.57% Accuracy on Track 1 (SAR), and +31.86% Accuracy on Track 2 (SAR+EO), demonstrating the potential of SCP-Label as post-processing. Finally, we win the championship both on Track1 and Track2 in the CVPR 2022 Perception Beyond the Visible Spectrum (PBVS) Workshop MAVOC Challenge. Our code is available at https://github.com/HowieChangchn/SCP-Label.
summary: Multi-modal aerial view object classification (MAVOC) in Automatic target recognition (ATR), although an important and challenging problem, has been under studied. This paper firstly finds that fine-grained data, class imbalance and various shooting conditions preclude the representational ability of general image classification. Moreover, the MAVOC dataset has scene aggregation characteristics. By exploiting these properties, we propose Scene Clustering Based Pseudo-labeling Strategy (SCP-Label), a simple yet effective method to employ in post-processing. The SCP-Label brings greater accuracy by assigning the same label to objects within the same scene while also mitigating bias and confusion with model ensembles. Its performance surpasses the official baseline by a large margin of +20.57% Accuracy on Track 1 (SAR), and +31.86% Accuracy on Track 2 (SAR+EO), demonstrating the potential of SCP-Label as post-processing. Finally, we win the championship both on Track1 and Track2 in the CVPR 2022 Perception Beyond the Visible Spectrum (PBVS) Workshop MAVOC Challenge. Our code is available at https://github.com/HowieChangchn/SCP-Label.
abstract_zh: 自动目标识别（ATR）中的多模态鸟瞰对象分类（MAVOC）虽然是一个重要且具有挑战性的问题，但一直在研究中。本文首先发现细粒度数据、类别不平衡和各种拍摄条件阻碍了一般图像分类的表征能力。此外，MAVOC数据集具有场景聚合特征。通过利用这些特性，我们提出了基于场景聚类的伪标签策略（SCP-Label），这是一种用于后处理的简单而有效的方法。 SCP 标签通过为同一场景中的对象分配相同的标签来提高准确性，同时减少模型集合的偏差和混乱。其性能在轨道 1 (SAR) 上的准确度大幅超过官方基线 +20.57%，在轨道 2 (SAR+EO) 上的准确度 +31.86%，充分证明了 SCP-Label 作为后处理的潜力。最终，我们在 CVPR 2022 Perception Beyond the Visible Spectrum (PBVS) Workshop MAVOC Challenge 中获得了 Track1 和 Track2 的冠军。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/HowieChangchn/SCP-Label 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于场景聚类的伪标签策略（SCP-Label），用于解决多模态航拍视角目标分类中的数据不平衡和场景特性利用不足问题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理细粒度数据、类别不平衡及多拍摄条件时表现不足，且未充分利用MAVOC数据集的场景聚合特性。
AI.method: 通过场景聚类分配相同标签并结合模型集成，设计后处理伪标签策略SCP-Label以提升分类准确性。
AI.result: 在CVPR 2022 PBVS Workshop挑战赛中，Track1和Track2准确率分别提升20.57%和31.86%，并获得双赛道冠军。
AI.conclusion: SCP-Label有效利用场景特性，显著提升多模态航拍目标分类性能，验证了其作为后处理方法的潜力。

search_text: Scene Clustering Based Pseudo-labeling Strategy for Multi-modal Aerial View Object Classification 基于场景聚类的多模态鸟瞰目标分类伪标记策略 Multi-modal aerial view object classification (MAVOC) in Automatic target recognition (ATR), although an important and challenging problem, has been under studied. This paper firstly finds that fine-grained data, class imbalance and various shooting conditions preclude the representational ability of general image classification. Moreover, the MAVOC dataset has scene aggregation characteristics. By exploiting these properties, we propose Scene Clustering Based Pseudo-labeling Strategy (SCP-Label), a simple yet effective method to employ in post-processing. The SCP-Label brings greater accuracy by assigning the same label to objects within the same scene while also mitigating bias and confusion with model ensembles. Its performance surpasses the official baseline by a large margin of +20.57% Accuracy on Track 1 (SAR), and +31.86% Accuracy on Track 2 (SAR+EO), demonstrating the potential of SCP-Label as post-processing. Finally, we win the championship both on Track1 and Track2 in the CVPR 2022 Perception Beyond the Visible Spectrum (PBVS) Workshop MAVOC Challenge. Our code is available at https://github.com/HowieChangchn/SCP-Label. 自动目标识别（ATR）中的多模态鸟瞰对象分类（MAVOC）虽然是一个重要且具有挑战性的问题，但一直在研究中。本文首先发现细粒度数据、类别不平衡和各种拍摄条件阻碍了一般图像分类的表征能力。此外，MAVOC数据集具有场景聚合特征。通过利用这些特性，我们提出了基于场景聚类的伪标签策略（SCP-Label），这是一种用于后处理的简单而有效的方法。 SCP 标签通过为同一场景中的对象分配相同的标签来提高准确性，同时减少模型集合的偏差和混乱。其性能在轨道 1 (SAR) 上的准确度大幅超过官方基线 +20.57%，在轨道 2 (SAR+EO) 上的准确度 +31.86%，充分证明了 SCP-Label 作为后处理的潜力。最终，我们在 CVPR 2022 Perception Beyond the Visible Spectrum (PBVS) Workshop MAVOC Challenge 中获得了 Track1 和 Track2 的冠军。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/HowieChangchn/SCP-Label 获取。 本文提出一种基于场景聚类的伪标签策略（SCP-Label），用于解决多模态航拍视角目标分类中的数据不平衡和场景特性利用不足问题。 Jun Yu,Hao Chang,Keda Lu,Liwen Zhang,Shenshen Du,Zhong Zhang cs.CV

authors_text: Jiahui Yu,Zirui Wang,Vijay Vasudevan,Legg Yeung,Mojtaba Seyedhosseini,Yonghui Wu
title: CoCa: Contrastive Captioners are Image-Text Foundation Models
title_zh: CoCa：对比字幕是图像文本基础模型
abstract: Exploring large-scale pretrained foundation models is of significant interest in computer vision because these models can be quickly transferred to many downstream tasks. This paper presents Contrastive Captioner (CoCa), a minimalist design to pretrain an image-text encoder-decoder foundation model jointly with contrastive loss and captioning loss, thereby subsuming model capabilities from contrastive approaches like CLIP and generative methods like SimVLM. In contrast to standard encoder-decoder transformers where all decoder layers attend to encoder outputs, CoCa omits cross-attention in the first half of decoder layers to encode unimodal text representations, and cascades the remaining decoder layers which cross-attend to the image encoder for multimodal image-text representations. We apply a contrastive loss between unimodal image and text embeddings, in addition to a captioning loss on the multimodal decoder outputs which predicts text tokens autoregressively. By sharing the same computational graph, the two training objectives are computed efficiently with minimal overhead. CoCa is pretrained end-to-end and from scratch on both web-scale alt-text data and annotated images by treating all labels simply as text, seamlessly unifying natural language supervision for representation learning. Empirically, CoCa achieves state-of-the-art performance with zero-shot transfer or minimal task-specific adaptation on a broad range of downstream tasks, spanning visual recognition (ImageNet, Kinetics-400/600/700, Moments-in-Time), crossmodal retrieval (MSCOCO, Flickr30K, MSR-VTT), multimodal understanding (VQA, SNLI-VE, NLVR2), and image captioning (MSCOCO, NoCaps). Notably on ImageNet classification, CoCa obtains 86.3% zero-shot top-1 accuracy, 90.6% with a frozen encoder and learned classification head, and new state-of-the-art 91.0% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet with a finetuned encoder.

This paper presents Contrastive Captioner (CoCa), a minimalist design to pretrain an image-text encoder-decoder foundation model jointly with contrastive loss and captioning loss, thereby subsuming model capabilities from contrastive approaches like CLIP and generative methods like SimVLM. In contrast to standard encoder-decoder transformers where all decoder layers attend to encoder outputs, CoCa omits cross-attention in the first half of decoder layers to encode unimodal text representations, and cascades the remaining decoder layers which cross-attend to the image encoder for multimodal image-text representations. We apply a contrastive loss between unimodal image and text embeddings, in addition to a captioning loss on the multimodal decoder outputs which predicts text tokens autoregressively. By sharing the same computational graph, the two training objectives are computed efficiently with minimal overhead. CoCa is pretrained end-to-end and from scratch on both web-scale alt-text data and annotated images by treating all labels simply as text, seamlessly unifying natural language supervision for representation learning. Empirically, CoCa achieves state-of-the-art performance with zero-shot transfer or minimal task-specific adaptation on a broad range of downstream tasks, spanning visual recognition (ImageNet, Kinetics-400/600/700, Moments-in-Time), crossmodal retrieval (MSCOCO, Flickr30K, MSR-VTT), multimodal understanding (VQA, SNLI-VE, NLVR2), and image captioning (MSCOCO, NoCaps). Notably on ImageNet classification, CoCa obtains 86.3% zero-shot top-1 accuracy, 90.6% with a frozen encoder and learned classification head, and new state-of-the-art 91.0% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet with a finetuned encoder.
abstract_zh: 探索大规模预训练基础模型在计算机视觉领域具有重要意义，因为这些模型可以快速转移到许多下游任务。本文提出了 Contrastive Captioner (CoCa)，这是一种极简设计，用于与对比损失和字幕损失联合预训练图像-文本编码器-解码器基础模型，从而包含来自 CLIP 等对比方法和 SimVLM 等生成方法的模型功能。与所有解码器层都参与编码器输出的标准编码器-解码器变换器相比，CoCa 省略了解码器层前半部分的交叉关注来编码单模态文本表示，并级联其余解码器层，交叉参与图像编码器以实现多模态图像文本表示。除了多模态解码器输出上的字幕损失（自回归预测文本标记）之外，我们还在单模态图像和文本嵌入之间应用对比损失。通过共享相同的计算图，可以以最小的开销有效地计算两个训练目标。 CoCa 通过将所有标签简单地视为文本，在网络规模的替代文本数据和带注释的图像上进行了端到端的预训练，无缝地统一了表示学习的自然语言监督。根据经验，CoCa 在各种下游任务上通过零镜头传输或最小的任务特定适应实现了最先进的性能，涵盖视觉识别（ImageNet、Kinetics-400/600/700、Moments-in-Time）、跨模态检索（MSCOCO、Flickr30K、MSR-VTT）、多模态理解（VQA、SNLI-VE、NLVR2）和图像字幕（MSCOCO，NoCaps）。值得注意的是，在 ImageNet 分类上，CoCa 获得了 86.3% 的零样本 top-1 准确率，在使用冻结编码器和学习分类头的情况下获得了 90.6% 的准确率，在使用微调编码器的 ImageNet 上获得了最先进的 91.0% top-1 准确率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CoCa，一种通过联合对比损失和字幕损失预训练图像-文本编码器-解码器基础模型的极简设计。
AI.motivation: 现有对比方法（如CLIP）和生成方法（如SimVLM）各自存在局限，需统一模型能力并提升效率。
AI.method: 在编码器-解码器结构中，前半段解码器层去除跨注意力以生成单模态文本表示，后半段通过跨注意力融合图像与文本信息。同时使用对比损失和自回归字幕损失，共享计算图高效训练。
AI.result: 在ImageNet分类等10+下游任务中取得SOTA结果，零样本准确率达86.3%，微调后达91.0%。
AI.conclusion: CoCa通过统一的双目标预训练策略，实现了跨模态理解与生成能力的高效融合。

title: Generalized Knowledge Distillation via Relationship Matching
title_zh: 通过关系匹配进行广义知识蒸馏
comment: This manuscript has been accepted by TPAMI
comments: This manuscript has been accepted by TPAMI
abstract: The knowledge of a well-trained deep neural network (a.k.a. the "teacher") is valuable for learning similar tasks. Knowledge distillation extracts knowledge from the teacher and integrates it with the target model (a.k.a. the "student"), which expands the student's knowledge and improves its learning efficacy. Instead of enforcing the teacher to work on the same task as the student, we borrow the knowledge from a teacher trained from a general label space -- in this "Generalized Knowledge Distillation (GKD)", the classes of the teacher and the student may be the same, completely different, or partially overlapped. We claim that the comparison ability between instances acts as an essential factor threading knowledge across tasks, and propose the RElationship FacIlitated Local cLassifiEr Distillation (REFILLED) approach, which decouples the GKD flow of the embedding and the top-layer classifier. In particular, different from reconciling the instance-label confidence between models, REFILLED requires the teacher to reweight the hard tuples pushed forward by the student and then matches the similarity comparison levels between instances. An embedding-induced classifier based on the teacher model supervises the student's classification confidence and adaptively emphasizes the most related supervision from the teacher. REFILLED demonstrates strong discriminative ability when the classes of the teacher vary from the same to a fully non-overlapped set w.r.t. the student. It also achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard knowledge distillation, one-step incremental learning, and few-shot learning tasks.
summary: The knowledge of a well-trained deep neural network (a.k.a. the "teacher") is valuable for learning similar tasks. Knowledge distillation extracts knowledge from the teacher and integrates it with the target model (a.k.a. the "student"), which expands the student's knowledge and improves its learning efficacy. Instead of enforcing the teacher to work on the same task as the student, we borrow the knowledge from a teacher trained from a general label space -- in this "Generalized Knowledge Distillation (GKD)", the classes of the teacher and the student may be the same, completely different, or partially overlapped. We claim that the comparison ability between instances acts as an essential factor threading knowledge across tasks, and propose the RElationship FacIlitated Local cLassifiEr Distillation (REFILLED) approach, which decouples the GKD flow of the embedding and the top-layer classifier. In particular, different from reconciling the instance-label confidence between models, REFILLED requires the teacher to reweight the hard tuples pushed forward by the student and then matches the similarity comparison levels between instances. An embedding-induced classifier based on the teacher model supervises the student's classification confidence and adaptively emphasizes the most related supervision from the teacher. REFILLED demonstrates strong discriminative ability when the classes of the teacher vary from the same to a fully non-overlapped set w.r.t. the student. It also achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard knowledge distillation, one-step incremental learning, and few-shot learning tasks.
abstract_zh: 训练有素的深度神经网络（又称“老师”）的知识对于学习类似的任务很有价值。知识蒸馏从教师那里提取知识，并将其与目标模型（又称“学生”）集成，从而扩展了学生的知识并提高了其学习效率。我们不是强迫教师与学生完成相同的任务，而是借用从通用标签空间训练过的教师的知识——在这种“广义知识蒸馏（GKD）”中，教师和学生的班级可能相同、完全不同或部分重叠。我们声称实例之间的比较能力是跨任务线程化知识的重要因素，并提出了关系促进局部分类器蒸馏（REFILLED）方法，该方法将嵌入和顶层分类器的 GKD 流程解耦。特别是，与协调模型之间的实例标签置信度不同，REFILLED 要求教师重新加权学生推送的硬元组，然后匹配实例之间的相似性比较级别。基于教师模型的嵌入诱导分类器监督学生的分类置信度，并自适应地强调来自教师的最相关的监督。当教师的课程从相同到完全不重叠的集合变化时，REFILLED 表现出很强的辨别能力。学生。它还在标准知识蒸馏、一步增量学习和少样本学习任务方面实现了最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种广义知识蒸馏方法（GKD），通过关系匹配实现教师模型与学生模型在不同类别空间下的知识迁移。
AI.motivation: 传统知识蒸馏要求教师模型与学生模型任务一致，限制了跨任务知识迁移的灵活性，而实际场景中类别可能存在差异或部分重叠。
AI.method: 提出REFILLED方法，解耦嵌入层与分类器的蒸馏流程，通过教师模型重加权学生生成的困难样本对，匹配实例间相似度关系，并利用教师引导的嵌入分类器动态强化相关监督。
AI.result: 在标准知识蒸馏、单步增量学习和少样本学习任务中均取得最先进性能，尤其在教师学生类别完全不重叠时展现强判别能力。
AI.conclusion: 关系匹配机制有效突破了传统蒸馏的类别对齐限制，为跨任务知识迁移提供了通用解决方案。

search_text: Generalized Knowledge Distillation via Relationship Matching 通过关系匹配进行广义知识蒸馏 The knowledge of a well-trained deep neural network (a.k.a. the "teacher") is valuable for learning similar tasks. Knowledge distillation extracts knowledge from the teacher and integrates it with the target model (a.k.a. the "student"), which expands the student's knowledge and improves its learning efficacy. Instead of enforcing the teacher to work on the same task as the student, we borrow the knowledge from a teacher trained from a general label space -- in this "Generalized Knowledge Distillation (GKD)", the classes of the teacher and the student may be the same, completely different, or partially overlapped. We claim that the comparison ability between instances acts as an essential factor threading knowledge across tasks, and propose the RElationship FacIlitated Local cLassifiEr Distillation (REFILLED) approach, which decouples the GKD flow of the embedding and the top-layer classifier. In particular, different from reconciling the instance-label confidence between models, REFILLED requires the teacher to reweight the hard tuples pushed forward by the student and then matches the similarity comparison levels between instances. An embedding-induced classifier based on the teacher model supervises the student's classification confidence and adaptively emphasizes the most related supervision from the teacher. REFILLED demonstrates strong discriminative ability when the classes of the teacher vary from the same to a fully non-overlapped set w.r.t. the student. It also achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard knowledge distillation, one-step incremental learning, and few-shot learning tasks. 训练有素的深度神经网络（又称“老师”）的知识对于学习类似的任务很有价值。知识蒸馏从教师那里提取知识，并将其与目标模型（又称“学生”）集成，从而扩展了学生的知识并提高了其学习效率。我们不是强迫教师与学生完成相同的任务，而是借用从通用标签空间训练过的教师的知识——在这种“广义知识蒸馏（GKD）”中，教师和学生的班级可能相同、完全不同或部分重叠。我们声称实例之间的比较能力是跨任务线程化知识的重要因素，并提出了关系促进局部分类器蒸馏（REFILLED）方法，该方法将嵌入和顶层分类器的 GKD 流程解耦。特别是，与协调模型之间的实例标签置信度不同，REFILLED 要求教师重新加权学生推送的硬元组，然后匹配实例之间的相似性比较级别。基于教师模型的嵌入诱导分类器监督学生的分类置信度，并自适应地强调来自教师的最相关的监督。当教师的课程从相同到完全不重叠的集合变化时，REFILLED 表现出很强的辨别能力。学生。它还在标准知识蒸馏、一步增量学习和少样本学习任务方面实现了最先进的性能。 本文提出了一种广义知识蒸馏方法（GKD），通过关系匹配实现教师模型与学生模型在不同类别空间下的知识迁移。 Han-Jia Ye,Su Lu,De-Chuan Zhan cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Xue Bin Peng,Yunrong Guo,Lina Halper,Sergey Levine,Sanja Fidler
title: ASE: Large-Scale Reusable Adversarial Skill Embeddings for Physically Simulated Characters
title_zh: ASE：用于物理模拟角色的大规模可重用对抗技能嵌入
abstract: The incredible feats of athleticism demonstrated by humans are made possible in part by a vast repertoire of general-purpose motor skills, acquired through years of practice and experience. These skills not only enable humans to perform complex tasks, but also provide powerful priors for guiding their behaviors when learning new tasks. This is in stark contrast to what is common practice in physics-based character animation, where control policies are most typically trained from scratch for each task. In this work, we present a large-scale data-driven framework for learning versatile and reusable skill embeddings for physically simulated characters. Our approach combines techniques from adversarial imitation learning and unsupervised reinforcement learning to develop skill embeddings that produce life-like behaviors, while also providing an easy to control representation for use on new downstream tasks. Our models can be trained using large datasets of unstructured motion clips, without requiring any task-specific annotation or segmentation of the motion data. By leveraging a massively parallel GPU-based simulator, we are able to train skill embeddings using over a decade of simulated experiences, enabling our model to learn a rich and versatile repertoire of skills. We show that a single pre-trained model can be effectively applied to perform a diverse set of new tasks. Our system also allows users to specify tasks through simple reward functions, and the skill embedding then enables the character to automatically synthesize complex and naturalistic strategies in order to achieve the task objectives.

These skills not only enable humans to perform complex tasks, but also provide powerful priors for guiding their behaviors when learning new tasks. This is in stark contrast to what is common practice in physics-based character animation, where control policies are most typically trained from scratch for each task. In this work, we present a large-scale data-driven framework for learning versatile and reusable skill embeddings for physically simulated characters. Our approach combines techniques from adversarial imitation learning and unsupervised reinforcement learning to develop skill embeddings that produce life-like behaviors, while also providing an easy to control representation for use on new downstream tasks. Our models can be trained using large datasets of unstructured motion clips, without requiring any task-specific annotation or segmentation of the motion data. By leveraging a massively parallel GPU-based simulator, we are able to train skill embeddings using over a decade of simulated experiences, enabling our model to learn a rich and versatile repertoire of skills. We show that a single pre-trained model can be effectively applied to perform a diverse set of new tasks. Our system also allows users to specify tasks through simple reward functions, and the skill embedding then enables the character to automatically synthesize complex and naturalistic strategies in order to achieve the task objectives.
abstract_zh: 人类所展现出的令人难以置信的运动能力在一定程度上是通过多年的练习和经验获得的大量通用运动技能得以实现的。这些技能不仅使人类能够执行复杂的任务，而且还为学习新任务时指导他们的行为提供了强大的先验。这与基于物理的角色动画的常见做法形成鲜明对比，其中控制策略通常是针对每个任务从头开始训练的。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个大规模数据驱动的框架，用于学习物理模拟角色的多功能且可重用的技能嵌入。我们的方法结合了对抗性模仿学习和无监督强化学习的技术来开发产生逼真行为的技能嵌入，同时还提供了一种易于控制的表示以用于新的下游任务。我们的模型可以使用非结构化运动剪辑的大型数据集进行训练，而不需要任何特定于任务的注释或运动数据的分割。通过利用基于 GPU 的大规模并行模拟器，我们能够利用十多年的模拟经验来训练技能嵌入，使我们的模型能够学习丰富且通用的技能。我们证明，单个预训练模型可以有效地应用于执行一组不同的新任务。我们的系统还允许用户通过简单的奖励函数来指定任务，然后技能嵌入使角色能够自动合成复杂且自然的策略以实现任务目标。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ASE框架，通过大规模数据驱动学习物理模拟角色的可复用对抗技能嵌入，无需任务特定标注即可完成多样化任务。
AI.motivation: 传统物理角色动画需为每个任务单独训练控制策略效率低下，而人类通过通用运动技能实现复杂任务，需开发类似可复用的技能表示。
AI.method: 结合对抗模仿学习与无监督强化学习，利用未结构化动作数据集训练技能嵌入，借助GPU模拟器生成十年级模拟经验，通过奖励函数指定任务目标。
AI.result: 预训练模型可有效适配多种新任务，角色能自动合成自然策略达成目标，验证了技能嵌入的泛化能力与可控性。
AI.conclusion: ASE方法为物理模拟角色提供了通用技能表示，显著提升行为灵活性与训练效率，为下游任务迁移奠定基础。

authors_text: Ayush Tripathi,Arnab Kumar Mondal,Lalan Kumar,Prathosh A. P
title: ImAiR: Airwriting Recognition framework using Image Representation of IMU Signals
title_zh: ImAiR：使用 IMU 信号图像表示的空中书写识别框架
abstract: The problem of Airwriting Recognition is focused on identifying letters written by movement of finger in free space. It is a type of gesture recognition where the dictionary corresponds to letters in a specific language. In particular, airwriting recognition using sensor data from wrist-worn devices can be used as a medium of user input for applications in Human-Computer Interaction (HCI). Recognition of in-air trajectories using such wrist-worn devices is limited in literature and forms the basis of the current work. In this paper, we propose an airwriting recognition framework by first encoding the time-series data obtained from a wearable Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) on the wrist as images and then utilizing deep learning-based models for identifying the written alphabets. The signals recorded from 3-axis accelerometer and gyroscope in IMU are encoded as images using different techniques such as Self Similarity Matrix (SSM), Gramian Angular Field (GAF) and Markov Transition Field (MTF) to form two sets of 3-channel images. These are then fed to two separate classification models and letter prediction is made based on an average of the class conditional probabilities obtained from the two models. Several standard model architectures for image classification such as variants of ResNet, DenseNet, VGGNet, AlexNet and GoogleNet have been utilized. Experiments performed on two publicly available datasets demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed strategy. The code for our implementation will be made available at https://github.com/ayushayt/ImAiR.
summary: The problem of Airwriting Recognition is focused on identifying letters written by movement of finger in free space. It is a type of gesture recognition where the dictionary corresponds to letters in a specific language. In particular, airwriting recognition using sensor data from wrist-worn devices can be used as a medium of user input for applications in Human-Computer Interaction (HCI). Recognition of in-air trajectories using such wrist-worn devices is limited in literature and forms the basis of the current work. In this paper, we propose an airwriting recognition framework by first encoding the time-series data obtained from a wearable Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) on the wrist as images and then utilizing deep learning-based models for identifying the written alphabets. The signals recorded from 3-axis accelerometer and gyroscope in IMU are encoded as images using different techniques such as Self Similarity Matrix (SSM), Gramian Angular Field (GAF) and Markov Transition Field (MTF) to form two sets of 3-channel images. These are then fed to two separate classification models and letter prediction is made based on an average of the class conditional probabilities obtained from the two models. Several standard model architectures for image classification such as variants of ResNet, DenseNet, VGGNet, AlexNet and GoogleNet have been utilized. Experiments performed on two publicly available datasets demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed strategy. The code for our implementation will be made available at https://github.com/ayushayt/ImAiR.
abstract_zh: 空中书写识别问题的重点是识别自由空间中手指运动书写的字母。这是一种手势识别，其中字典对应于特定语言中的字母。特别是，使用来自腕戴式设备的传感器数据的空中书写识别可以用作人机交互（HCI）应用程序的用户输入媒介。使用此类腕戴式设备识别空中轨迹的文献有限，但构成了当前工作的基础。在本文中，我们提出了一种空中书写识别框架，首先将从手腕上的可穿戴惯性测量单元（IMU）获得的时间序列数据编码为图像，然后利用基于深度学习的模型来识别书写字母。 IMU 中的 3 轴加速度计和陀螺仪记录的信号使用不同的技术（例如自相似矩阵（SSM）、格拉米安角场（GAF）和马尔可夫过渡场（MTF））编码为图像，形成两组 3 通道图像。然后将它们输入两个单独的分类模型，并根据从两个模型获得的类条件概率的平均值进行字母预测。已经使用了几种用于图像分类的标准模型架构，例如 ResNet、DenseNet、VGGNet、AlexNet 和 GoogleNet 的变体。在两个公开可用的数据集上进行的实验证明了所提出策略的有效性。我们的实现代码将在 https://github.com/ayushayt/ImAiR 上提供。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于惯性测量单元（IMU）信号图像表示的空中书写识别框架ImAiR，通过将IMU时序数据转换为图像并利用深度学习模型进行字母识别。
AI.motivation: 现有基于可穿戴设备的空中轨迹识别方法研究较少，需探索更有效的传感器数据处理与识别技术以提升人机交互的输入方式。
AI.method: 通过Self Similarity Matrix（SSM）、Gramian Angular Field（GAF）和Markov Transition Field（MTF）等技术将IMU的三轴加速度计和陀螺仪信号转换为双通道三通道图像，再使用ResNet、DenseNet等图像分类模型进行字母预测。
AI.result: 在两个公开数据集上的实验验证了该方法的有效性，分类准确率表现优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 将IMU时序数据转化为图像并结合深度学习模型可有效提升空中书写识别性能，代码已开源。

search_text: ImAiR: Airwriting Recognition framework using Image Representation of IMU Signals ImAiR：使用 IMU 信号图像表示的空中书写识别框架 The problem of Airwriting Recognition is focused on identifying letters written by movement of finger in free space. It is a type of gesture recognition where the dictionary corresponds to letters in a specific language. In particular, airwriting recognition using sensor data from wrist-worn devices can be used as a medium of user input for applications in Human-Computer Interaction (HCI). Recognition of in-air trajectories using such wrist-worn devices is limited in literature and forms the basis of the current work. In this paper, we propose an airwriting recognition framework by first encoding the time-series data obtained from a wearable Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) on the wrist as images and then utilizing deep learning-based models for identifying the written alphabets. The signals recorded from 3-axis accelerometer and gyroscope in IMU are encoded as images using different techniques such as Self Similarity Matrix (SSM), Gramian Angular Field (GAF) and Markov Transition Field (MTF) to form two sets of 3-channel images. These are then fed to two separate classification models and letter prediction is made based on an average of the class conditional probabilities obtained from the two models. Several standard model architectures for image classification such as variants of ResNet, DenseNet, VGGNet, AlexNet and GoogleNet have been utilized. Experiments performed on two publicly available datasets demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed strategy. The code for our implementation will be made available at https://github.com/ayushayt/ImAiR. 空中书写识别问题的重点是识别自由空间中手指运动书写的字母。这是一种手势识别，其中字典对应于特定语言中的字母。特别是，使用来自腕戴式设备的传感器数据的空中书写识别可以用作人机交互（HCI）应用程序的用户输入媒介。使用此类腕戴式设备识别空中轨迹的文献有限，但构成了当前工作的基础。在本文中，我们提出了一种空中书写识别框架，首先将从手腕上的可穿戴惯性测量单元（IMU）获得的时间序列数据编码为图像，然后利用基于深度学习的模型来识别书写字母。 IMU 中的 3 轴加速度计和陀螺仪记录的信号使用不同的技术（例如自相似矩阵（SSM）、格拉米安角场（GAF）和马尔可夫过渡场（MTF））编码为图像，形成两组 3 通道图像。然后将它们输入两个单独的分类模型，并根据从两个模型获得的类条件概率的平均值进行字母预测。已经使用了几种用于图像分类的标准模型架构，例如 ResNet、DenseNet、VGGNet、AlexNet 和 GoogleNet 的变体。在两个公开可用的数据集上进行的实验证明了所提出策略的有效性。我们的实现代码将在 https://github.com/ayushayt/ImAiR 上提供。 本文提出了一种基于惯性测量单元（IMU）信号图像表示的空中书写识别框架ImAiR，通过将IMU时序数据转换为图像并利用深度学习模型进行字母识别。 Ayush Tripathi,Arnab Kumar Mondal,Lalan Kumar,Prathosh A. P cs.HC

title: Self-Taught Metric Learning without Labels
title_zh: 无标签自学度量学习
abstract: We present a novel self-taught framework for unsupervised metric learning, which alternates between predicting class-equivalence relations between data through a moving average of an embedding model and learning the model with the predicted relations as pseudo labels. At the heart of our framework lies an algorithm that investigates contexts of data on the embedding space to predict their class-equivalence relations as pseudo labels. The algorithm enables efficient end-to-end training since it demands no off-the-shelf module for pseudo labeling. Also, the class-equivalence relations provide rich supervisory signals for learning an embedding space. On standard benchmarks for metric learning, it clearly outperforms existing unsupervised learning methods and sometimes even beats supervised learning models using the same backbone network. It is also applied to semi-supervised metric learning as a way of exploiting additional unlabeled data, and achieves the state of the art by boosting performance of supervised learning substantially.
summary: We present a novel self-taught framework for unsupervised metric learning, which alternates between predicting class-equivalence relations between data through a moving average of an embedding model and learning the model with the predicted relations as pseudo labels. At the heart of our framework lies an algorithm that investigates contexts of data on the embedding space to predict their class-equivalence relations as pseudo labels. The algorithm enables efficient end-to-end training since it demands no off-the-shelf module for pseudo labeling. Also, the class-equivalence relations provide rich supervisory signals for learning an embedding space. On standard benchmarks for metric learning, it clearly outperforms existing unsupervised learning methods and sometimes even beats supervised learning models using the same backbone network. It is also applied to semi-supervised metric learning as a way of exploiting additional unlabeled data, and achieves the state of the art by boosting performance of supervised learning substantially.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种用于无监督度量学习的新型自学框架，该框架交替通过嵌入模型的移动平均值预测数据之间的类等价关系，并使用预测关系作为伪标签来学习模型。我们框架的核心是一种算法，该算法研究嵌入空间上的数据上下文，以将其类等价关系预测为伪标签。该算法可以实现高效的端到端训练，因为它不需要现成的伪标记模块。此外，类等价关系为学习嵌入空间提供了丰富的监督信号。在度量学习的标准基准上，它明显优于现有的无监督学习方法，有时甚至击败使用相同骨干网络的监督学习模型。它还应用于半监督度量学习，作为利用额外未标记数据的一种方式，并通过大幅提高监督学习的性能来实现最先进的技术。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需标签的自监督度量学习框架，通过预测数据的类等价关系并利用伪标签进行训练。
AI.motivation: 传统度量学习依赖标签数据，而本文旨在探索无监督场景下如何通过自监督方式学习有效嵌入空间。
AI.method: 框架通过嵌入空间中的数据上下文预测类等价关系作为伪标签，结合移动平均模型进行端到端训练，无需额外模块。
AI.result: 在标准基准测试中显著优于现有无监督方法，部分情况下超越使用相同骨干网络的监督模型，并在半监督学习中达到SOTA。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为无监督和半监督度量学习提供了高效且有效的解决方案，证明了伪标签生成与嵌入优化的协同潜力。

authors_text: Runsheng Xu,Zhengzhong Tu,Yuanqi Du,Xiaoyu Dong,Jinlong Li,Zibo Meng,Jiaqi Ma,Alan Bovik,Hongkai Yu
title: Pik-Fix: Restoring and Colorizing Old Photos
title_zh: Pik-Fix：旧照片的修复和着色
comment: WACV 2022; code: https://github.com/DerrickXuNu/Pik-Fix. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2202.02606
comments: WACV 2022; code: https://github.com/DerrickXuNu/Pik-Fix. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2202.02606
abstract: Restoring and inpainting the visual memories that are present, but often impaired, in old photos remains an intriguing but unsolved research topic. Decades-old photos often suffer from severe and commingled degradation such as cracks, defocus, and color-fading, which are difficult to treat individually and harder to repair when they interact. Deep learning presents a plausible avenue, but the lack of large-scale datasets of old photos makes addressing this restoration task very challenging. Here we present a novel reference-based end-to-end learning framework that is able to both repair and colorize old, degraded pictures. Our proposed framework consists of three modules: a restoration sub-network that conducts restoration from degradations, a similarity network that performs color histogram matching and color transfer, and a colorization subnet that learns to predict the chroma elements of images conditioned on chromatic reference signals. The overall system makes uses of color histogram priors from reference images, which greatly reduces the need for large-scale training data. We have also created a first-of-a-kind public dataset of real old photos that are paired with ground truth ''pristine'' photos that have been manually restored by PhotoShop experts. We conducted extensive experiments on this dataset and synthetic datasets, and found that our method significantly outperforms previous state-of-the-art models using both qualitative comparisons and quantitative measurements. The code is available at https://github.com/DerrickXuNu/Pik-Fix.
summary: Restoring and inpainting the visual memories that are present, but often impaired, in old photos remains an intriguing but unsolved research topic. Decades-old photos often suffer from severe and commingled degradation such as cracks, defocus, and color-fading, which are difficult to treat individually and harder to repair when they interact. Deep learning presents a plausible avenue, but the lack of large-scale datasets of old photos makes addressing this restoration task very challenging. Here we present a novel reference-based end-to-end learning framework that is able to both repair and colorize old, degraded pictures. Our proposed framework consists of three modules: a restoration sub-network that conducts restoration from degradations, a similarity network that performs color histogram matching and color transfer, and a colorization subnet that learns to predict the chroma elements of images conditioned on chromatic reference signals. The overall system makes uses of color histogram priors from reference images, which greatly reduces the need for large-scale training data. We have also created a first-of-a-kind public dataset of real old photos that are paired with ground truth ''pristine'' photos that have been manually restored by PhotoShop experts. We conducted extensive experiments on this dataset and synthetic datasets, and found that our method significantly outperforms previous state-of-the-art models using both qualitative comparisons and quantitative measurements. The code is available at https://github.com/DerrickXuNu/Pik-Fix.
abstract_zh: 恢复和修复旧照片中存在但经常受损的视觉记忆仍然是一个有趣但尚未解决的研究课题。几十年前的照片经常会出现严重的混合退化，例如裂纹、散焦和褪色，这些问题很难单独处理，而且当它们相互作用时更难以修复。深度学习提供了一条看似可行的途径，但缺乏大规模的旧照片数据集使得解决这一修复任务非常具有挑战性。在这里，我们提出了一种新颖的基于参考的端到端学习框架，它能够修复旧的、退化的图片并为其着色。我们提出的框架由三个模块组成：一个用于从退化中进行恢复的恢复子网络，一个执行颜色直方图匹配和颜色传输的相似性网络，以及一个学习预测以色度参考信号为条件的图像色度元素的着色子网。整个系统利用参考图像的颜色直方图先验，这大大减少了对大规模训练数据的需求。我们还创建了首个真实老照片的公共数据集，这些照片与由 PhotoShop 专家手动修复的真实“原始”照片配对。我们对该数据集和合成数据集进行了广泛的实验，发现我们的方法在使用定性比较和定量测量方面显着优于以前最先进的模型。该代码可从 https://github.com/DerrickXuNu/Pik-Fix 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于参考的端到端学习框架Pik-Fix，用于修复和着色老照片。
AI.motivation: 老照片常常受到裂缝、失焦和褪色等严重且复杂的退化影响，传统的单个修复方法难以有效处理这些问题，而深度学习方法又因缺乏大规模数据集而面临挑战。
AI.method: 该方法包括三个模块：一个用于修复退化的子网络、一个用于颜色直方图匹配和颜色迁移的相似性网络，以及一个基于颜色参考信号预测图像色度元素的颜色化子网络。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在真实和合成数据集上显著优于现有最先进的模型，并且提供了首个公开的老照片数据集。
AI.conclusion: Pik-Fix框架通过利用颜色直方图先验信息，有效减少了对大规模训练数据的需求，并在老照片修复和着色任务中取得了优异性能。

tldr: 本文提出了一种基于参考的端到端学习框架Pik-Fix，用于修复和着色老照片。
motivation: 老照片常常受到裂缝、失焦和褪色等严重且复杂的退化影响，传统的单个修复方法难以有效处理这些问题，而深度学习方法又因缺乏大规模数据集而面临挑战。
method: 该方法包括三个模块：一个用于修复退化的子网络、一个用于颜色直方图匹配和颜色迁移的相似性网络，以及一个基于颜色参考信号预测图像色度元素的颜色化子网络。
result: 实验表明，该方法在真实和合成数据集上显著优于现有最先进的模型，并且提供了首个公开的老照片数据集。
conclusion: Pik-Fix框架通过利用颜色直方图先验信息，有效减少了对大规模训练数据的需求，并在老照片修复和着色任务中取得了优异性能。
search_text: Pik-Fix: Restoring and Colorizing Old Photos Pik-Fix：旧照片的修复和着色 Restoring and inpainting the visual memories that are present, but often impaired, in old photos remains an intriguing but unsolved research topic. Decades-old photos often suffer from severe and commingled degradation such as cracks, defocus, and color-fading, which are difficult to treat individually and harder to repair when they interact. Deep learning presents a plausible avenue, but the lack of large-scale datasets of old photos makes addressing this restoration task very challenging. Here we present a novel reference-based end-to-end learning framework that is able to both repair and colorize old, degraded pictures. Our proposed framework consists of three modules: a restoration sub-network that conducts restoration from degradations, a similarity network that performs color histogram matching and color transfer, and a colorization subnet that learns to predict the chroma elements of images conditioned on chromatic reference signals. The overall system makes uses of color histogram priors from reference images, which greatly reduces the need for large-scale training data. We have also created a first-of-a-kind public dataset of real old photos that are paired with ground truth ''pristine'' photos that have been manually restored by PhotoShop experts. We conducted extensive experiments on this dataset and synthetic datasets, and found that our method significantly outperforms previous state-of-the-art models using both qualitative comparisons and quantitative measurements. The code is available at https://github.com/DerrickXuNu/Pik-Fix. 恢复和修复旧照片中存在但经常受损的视觉记忆仍然是一个有趣但尚未解决的研究课题。几十年前的照片经常会出现严重的混合退化，例如裂纹、散焦和褪色，这些问题很难单独处理，而且当它们相互作用时更难以修复。深度学习提供了一条看似可行的途径，但缺乏大规模的旧照片数据集使得解决这一修复任务非常具有挑战性。在这里，我们提出了一种新颖的基于参考的端到端学习框架，它能够修复旧的、退化的图片并为其着色。我们提出的框架由三个模块组成：一个用于从退化中进行恢复的恢复子网络，一个执行颜色直方图匹配和颜色传输的相似性网络，以及一个学习预测以色度参考信号为条件的图像色度元素的着色子网。整个系统利用参考图像的颜色直方图先验，这大大减少了对大规模训练数据的需求。我们还创建了首个真实老照片的公共数据集，这些照片与由 PhotoShop 专家手动修复的真实“原始”照片配对。我们对该数据集和合成数据集进行了广泛的实验，发现我们的方法在使用定性比较和定量测量方面显着优于以前最先进的模型。该代码可从 https://github.com/DerrickXuNu/Pik-Fix 获取。 本文提出了一种基于参考的端到端学习框架Pik-Fix，用于修复和着色老照片。 Runsheng Xu,Zhengzhong Tu,Yuanqi Du,Xiaoyu Dong,Jinlong Li,Zibo Meng,Jiaqi Ma,Alan Bovik,Hongkai Yu cs.CV,cs.AI

title: Go Back in Time: Generating Flashbacks in Stories with Event Temporal Prompts
title_zh: 回到过去：使用事件时间提示在故事中生成闪回
comment: Accepted by the main conference proceedings of NAACL 2022
comments: Accepted by the main conference proceedings of NAACL 2022
abstract: Stories or narratives are comprised of a sequence of events. To compose interesting stories, professional writers often leverage a creative writing technique called flashback that inserts past events into current storylines as we commonly observe in novels and plays. However, it is challenging for machines to generate flashback as it requires a solid understanding of event temporal order (e.g. "feeling hungry" before "eat," not vice versa), and the creativity to arrange storylines so that earlier events do not always appear first in narrative order. Two major issues in existing systems that exacerbate the challenges: 1) temporal bias in pertaining and story datasets that leads to monotonic event temporal orders; 2) lack of explicit guidance that helps machines decide where to insert flashbacks. We propose to address these issues using structured storylines to encode events and their pair-wise temporal relations (before, after and vague) as temporal prompts that guide how stories should unfold temporally. We leverage a Plan-and-Write framework enhanced by reinforcement learning to generate storylines and stories end-to-end. Evaluation results show that the proposed method can generate more interesting stories with flashbacks while maintaining textual diversity, fluency, and temporal coherence.
summary: Stories or narratives are comprised of a sequence of events. To compose interesting stories, professional writers often leverage a creative writing technique called flashback that inserts past events into current storylines as we commonly observe in novels and plays. However, it is challenging for machines to generate flashback as it requires a solid understanding of event temporal order (e.g. "feeling hungry" before "eat," not vice versa), and the creativity to arrange storylines so that earlier events do not always appear first in narrative order. Two major issues in existing systems that exacerbate the challenges: 1) temporal bias in pertaining and story datasets that leads to monotonic event temporal orders; 2) lack of explicit guidance that helps machines decide where to insert flashbacks. We propose to address these issues using structured storylines to encode events and their pair-wise temporal relations (before, after and vague) as temporal prompts that guide how stories should unfold temporally. We leverage a Plan-and-Write framework enhanced by reinforcement learning to generate storylines and stories end-to-end. Evaluation results show that the proposed method can generate more interesting stories with flashbacks while maintaining textual diversity, fluency, and temporal coherence.
abstract_zh: 故事或叙述由一系列事件组成。为了撰写有趣的故事，专业作家经常利用一种称为闪回的创造性写作技巧，将过去的事件插入当前的故事情节中，就像我们在小说和戏剧中常见的那样。然而，机器生成闪回具有挑战性，因为它需要对事件时间顺序有充分的理解（例如，在“吃饭”之前“感到饥饿”，而不是相反），并且需要创造性地安排故事情节，以便较早的事件并不总是按照叙事顺序首先出现。现有系统中的两个主要问题加剧了挑战：1）相关数据集和故事数据集中的时间偏差导致事件时间顺序单调； 2）缺乏明确的指导来帮助机器决定在哪里插入闪回。我们建议使用结构化故事情节来编码事件及其成对的时间关系（之前、之后和模糊）作为时间提示来解决这些问题，指导故事如何在时间上展开。我们利用强化学习增强的规划和编写框架来生成端到端的故事情节和故事。评估结果表明，所提出的方法可以生成更有趣的闪回故事，同时保持文本多样性、流畅性和时间连贯性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于事件时间提示的方法，用于在故事生成中有效插入闪回内容。
AI.motivation: 现有故事生成系统存在时间顺序单调和缺乏闪回插入指导的问题，导致生成的故事缺乏创意和时间多样性。
AI.method: 通过结构化情节编码事件的时间关系（前、后、模糊），结合强化学习的Plan-and-Write框架生成故事。
AI.result: 实验表明方法能生成更具吸引力的故事，同时保持文本多样性、流畅性和时间连贯性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过显式时间提示和强化学习有效解决了闪回生成中的时间顺序和创造性问题。

title: Virtual Analog Modeling of Distortion Circuits Using Neural Ordinary Differential Equations
title_zh: 使用神经常微分方程对失真电路进行虚拟模拟建模
comment: 8 pages, 10 figures, accepted for DAFx 2022 conference, for associated audio examples, see https://thewolfsound.com/publications/dafx2022/
comments: 8 pages, 10 figures, accepted for DAFx 2022 conference, for associated audio examples, see https://thewolfsound.com/publications/dafx2022/
abstract: Recent research in deep learning has shown that neural networks can learn differential equations governing dynamical systems. In this paper, we adapt this concept to Virtual Analog (VA) modeling to learn the ordinary differential equations (ODEs) governing the first-order and the second-order diode clipper. The proposed models achieve performance comparable to state-of-the-art recurrent neural networks (RNNs) albeit using fewer parameters. We show that this approach does not require oversampling and allows to increase the sampling rate after the training has completed, which results in increased accuracy. Using a sophisticated numerical solver allows to increase the accuracy at the cost of slower processing. ODEs learned this way do not require closed forms but are still physically interpretable.
summary: Recent research in deep learning has shown that neural networks can learn differential equations governing dynamical systems. In this paper, we adapt this concept to Virtual Analog (VA) modeling to learn the ordinary differential equations (ODEs) governing the first-order and the second-order diode clipper. The proposed models achieve performance comparable to state-of-the-art recurrent neural networks (RNNs) albeit using fewer parameters. We show that this approach does not require oversampling and allows to increase the sampling rate after the training has completed, which results in increased accuracy. Using a sophisticated numerical solver allows to increase the accuracy at the cost of slower processing. ODEs learned this way do not require closed forms but are still physically interpretable.
abstract_zh: 深度学习的最新研究表明，神经网络可以学习控制动力系统的微分方程。在本文中，我们将此概念应用于虚拟模拟（VA）建模，以学习控制一阶和二阶二极管限幅器的常微分方程（ODE）。尽管使用的参数较少，但所提出的模型实现了与最先进的循环神经网络（RNN）相当的性能。我们证明这种方法不需要过采样，并且允许在训练完成后增加采样率，从而提高准确性。使用复杂的数值求解器可以提高精度，但代价是处理速度较慢。通过这种方式学习的常微分方程不需要封闭形式，但仍然可以进行物理解释。

AI.tldr: 本文利用神经常微分方程（Neural ODE）对二极管限幅器电路进行虚拟模拟，学习其动态系统方程以实现高效建模。
AI.motivation: 传统虚拟模拟依赖复杂数学推导，而深度学习能自动学习微分方程，从而简化建模过程并提升性能。
AI.method: 采用神经网络学习一阶/二阶二极管限幅器的ODE，通过数值求解器优化精度，对比传统RNN模型。
AI.result: 参数量更少的情况下性能与RNN相当，无需过采样且支持训练后提升采样率，ODE具有物理可解释性。
AI.conclusion: 神经ODE为虚拟模拟提供新方法，在保持精度的同时降低复杂度，未来可扩展至更多电路类型。

authors_text: Cheng-Yen Yang,Zhongyu Jiang,Shih-Yu Gu,Jenq-Neng Hwang,Jang-Hee Yoo
title: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation Learning for Hierarchical Infant Pose Recognition with Synthetic Data
title_zh: 使用合成数据进行分层婴儿姿势识别的无监督域适应学习
comment: Accepted as a conference paper at ICME 2022
comments: Accepted as a conference paper at ICME 2022
abstract: The Alberta Infant Motor Scale (AIMS) is a well-known assessment scheme that evaluates the gross motor development of infants by recording the number of specific poses achieved. With the aid of the image-based pose recognition model, the AIMS evaluation procedure can be shortened and automated, providing early diagnosis or indicator of potential developmental disorder. Due to limited public infant-related datasets, many works use the SMIL-based method to generate synthetic infant images for training. However, this domain mismatch between real and synthetic training samples often leads to performance degradation during inference. In this paper, we present a CNN-based model which takes any infant image as input and predicts the coarse and fine-level pose labels. The model consists of an image branch and a pose branch, which respectively generates the coarse-level logits facilitated by the unsupervised domain adaptation and the 3D keypoints using the HRNet with SMPLify optimization. Then the outputs of these branches will be sent into the hierarchical pose recognition module to estimate the fine-level pose labels. We also collect and label a new AIMS dataset, which contains 750 real and 4000 synthetic infants images with AIMS pose labels. Our experimental results show that the proposed method can significantly align the distribution of synthetic and real-world datasets, thus achieving accurate performance on fine-grained infant pose recognition.
summary: The Alberta Infant Motor Scale (AIMS) is a well-known assessment scheme that evaluates the gross motor development of infants by recording the number of specific poses achieved. With the aid of the image-based pose recognition model, the AIMS evaluation procedure can be shortened and automated, providing early diagnosis or indicator of potential developmental disorder. Due to limited public infant-related datasets, many works use the SMIL-based method to generate synthetic infant images for training. However, this domain mismatch between real and synthetic training samples often leads to performance degradation during inference. In this paper, we present a CNN-based model which takes any infant image as input and predicts the coarse and fine-level pose labels. The model consists of an image branch and a pose branch, which respectively generates the coarse-level logits facilitated by the unsupervised domain adaptation and the 3D keypoints using the HRNet with SMPLify optimization. Then the outputs of these branches will be sent into the hierarchical pose recognition module to estimate the fine-level pose labels. We also collect and label a new AIMS dataset, which contains 750 real and 4000 synthetic infants images with AIMS pose labels. Our experimental results show that the proposed method can significantly align the distribution of synthetic and real-world datasets, thus achieving accurate performance on fine-grained infant pose recognition.
abstract_zh: 艾伯塔婴儿运动量表（AIMS）是一种众所周知的评估方案，通过记录所达到的特定姿势的数量来评估婴儿的粗大运动发育。借助基于图像的姿势识别模型，可以缩短 AIMS 评估程序并实现自动化，从而提供潜在发育障碍的早期诊断或指示。由于与婴儿相关的公共数据集有限，许多作品使用基于 SMIL 的方法来生成合成婴儿图像进行训练。然而，真实训练样本和合成训练样本之间的域不匹配通常会导致推理过程中的性能下降。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于 CNN 的模型，该模型将任何婴儿图像作为输入并预测粗略和精细级别的姿势标签。该模型由图像分支和姿态分支组成，分别使用 HRNet 和 SMPLify 优化，生成由无监督域自适应促进的粗级逻辑和 3D 关键点。然后这些分支的输出将被发送到分层姿势识别模块以估计精细级别的姿势标签。我们还收集并标记了一个新的 AIMS 数据集，其中包含 750 张真实婴儿图像和 4000 张带有 AIMS 姿势标签的合成婴儿图像。我们的实验结果表明，所提出的方法可以显着对齐合成数据集和真实数据集的分布，从而实现细粒度婴儿姿势识别的准确性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于卷积神经网络的层次化婴儿姿态识别模型，通过无监督域适应技术解决合成数据与真实数据间的分布差异问题。
AI.motivation: 由于真实婴儿数据集稀缺，现有研究依赖合成数据训练模型，但合成数据与真实场景存在域偏移，导致姿态识别性能下降，需提升跨域适应能力以实现准确的婴儿运动发育评估。
AI.method: 设计包含图像分支和姿态分支的双分支CNN模型，图像分支通过无监督域适应生成粗粒度姿态标签，姿态分支结合HRNet与SMPLify优化输出3D关键点，最终通过层次化模块融合特征实现细粒度姿态识别，并构建包含750张真实图像和4000张合成图像的新数据集。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法有效对齐合成数据与真实数据分布，在细粒度婴儿姿态识别任务中取得准确性能，验证了跨域适应的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 提出的层次化模型通过域适应技术解决了合成数据与真实场景的分布差异问题，为自动化婴儿运动发育评估提供了可靠的技术方案。

search_text: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation Learning for Hierarchical Infant Pose Recognition with Synthetic Data 使用合成数据进行分层婴儿姿势识别的无监督域适应学习 The Alberta Infant Motor Scale (AIMS) is a well-known assessment scheme that evaluates the gross motor development of infants by recording the number of specific poses achieved. With the aid of the image-based pose recognition model, the AIMS evaluation procedure can be shortened and automated, providing early diagnosis or indicator of potential developmental disorder. Due to limited public infant-related datasets, many works use the SMIL-based method to generate synthetic infant images for training. However, this domain mismatch between real and synthetic training samples often leads to performance degradation during inference. In this paper, we present a CNN-based model which takes any infant image as input and predicts the coarse and fine-level pose labels. The model consists of an image branch and a pose branch, which respectively generates the coarse-level logits facilitated by the unsupervised domain adaptation and the 3D keypoints using the HRNet with SMPLify optimization. Then the outputs of these branches will be sent into the hierarchical pose recognition module to estimate the fine-level pose labels. We also collect and label a new AIMS dataset, which contains 750 real and 4000 synthetic infants images with AIMS pose labels. Our experimental results show that the proposed method can significantly align the distribution of synthetic and real-world datasets, thus achieving accurate performance on fine-grained infant pose recognition. 艾伯塔婴儿运动量表（AIMS）是一种众所周知的评估方案，通过记录所达到的特定姿势的数量来评估婴儿的粗大运动发育。借助基于图像的姿势识别模型，可以缩短 AIMS 评估程序并实现自动化，从而提供潜在发育障碍的早期诊断或指示。由于与婴儿相关的公共数据集有限，许多作品使用基于 SMIL 的方法来生成合成婴儿图像进行训练。然而，真实训练样本和合成训练样本之间的域不匹配通常会导致推理过程中的性能下降。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于 CNN 的模型，该模型将任何婴儿图像作为输入并预测粗略和精细级别的姿势标签。该模型由图像分支和姿态分支组成，分别使用 HRNet 和 SMPLify 优化，生成由无监督域自适应促进的粗级逻辑和 3D 关键点。然后这些分支的输出将被发送到分层姿势识别模块以估计精细级别的姿势标签。我们还收集并标记了一个新的 AIMS 数据集，其中包含 750 张真实婴儿图像和 4000 张带有 AIMS 姿势标签的合成婴儿图像。我们的实验结果表明，所提出的方法可以显着对齐合成数据集和真实数据集的分布，从而实现细粒度婴儿姿势识别的准确性能。 本文提出一种基于卷积神经网络的层次化婴儿姿态识别模型，通过无监督域适应技术解决合成数据与真实数据间的分布差异问题。 Cheng-Yen Yang,Zhongyu Jiang,Shih-Yu Gu,Jenq-Neng Hwang,Jang-Hee Yoo cs.CV

title: Improving Multi-Document Summarization through Referenced Flexible Extraction with Credit-Awareness
title_zh: 通过具有信用意识的参考灵活提取来改进多文档摘要
abstract: A notable challenge in Multi-Document Summarization (MDS) is the extremely-long length of the input. In this paper, we present an extract-then-abstract Transformer framework to overcome the problem. Specifically, we leverage pre-trained language models to construct a hierarchical extractor for salient sentence selection across documents and an abstractor for rewriting the selected contents as summaries. However, learning such a framework is challenging since the optimal contents for the abstractor are generally unknown. Previous works typically create pseudo extraction oracle to enable the supervised learning for both the extractor and the abstractor. Nevertheless, we argue that the performance of such methods could be restricted due to the insufficient information for prediction and inconsistent objectives between training and testing. To this end, we propose a loss weighting mechanism that makes the model aware of the unequal importance for the sentences not in the pseudo extraction oracle, and leverage the fine-tuned abstractor to generate summary references as auxiliary signals for learning the extractor. Moreover, we propose a reinforcement learning method that can efficiently apply to the extractor for harmonizing the optimization between training and testing. Experiment results show that our framework substantially outperforms strong baselines with comparable model sizes and achieves the best results on the Multi-News, Multi-XScience, and WikiCatSum corpora.
summary: A notable challenge in Multi-Document Summarization (MDS) is the extremely-long length of the input. In this paper, we present an extract-then-abstract Transformer framework to overcome the problem. Specifically, we leverage pre-trained language models to construct a hierarchical extractor for salient sentence selection across documents and an abstractor for rewriting the selected contents as summaries. However, learning such a framework is challenging since the optimal contents for the abstractor are generally unknown. Previous works typically create pseudo extraction oracle to enable the supervised learning for both the extractor and the abstractor. Nevertheless, we argue that the performance of such methods could be restricted due to the insufficient information for prediction and inconsistent objectives between training and testing. To this end, we propose a loss weighting mechanism that makes the model aware of the unequal importance for the sentences not in the pseudo extraction oracle, and leverage the fine-tuned abstractor to generate summary references as auxiliary signals for learning the extractor. Moreover, we propose a reinforcement learning method that can efficiently apply to the extractor for harmonizing the optimization between training and testing. Experiment results show that our framework substantially outperforms strong baselines with comparable model sizes and achieves the best results on the Multi-News, Multi-XScience, and WikiCatSum corpora.
abstract_zh: 多文档摘要 (MDS) 的一个显着挑战是输入的长度极长。在本文中，我们提出了一个提取然后抽象的 Transformer 框架来克服这个问题。具体来说，我们利用预先训练的语言模型构建一个分层提取器，用于跨文档选择显着句子，并构建一个抽象器，用于将所选内容重写为摘要。然而，学习这样的框架具有挑战性，因为抽象器的最佳内容通常是未知的。以前的工作通常创建伪提取预言机，以实现提取器和抽象器的监督学习。尽管如此，我们认为，由于预测信息不足以及训练和测试目标不一致，此类方法的性能可能会受到限制。为此，我们提出了一种损失加权机制，使模型意识到不在伪提取预言机中的句子的重要性不同，并利用微调的抽象器生成摘要参考作为学习提取器的辅助信号。此外，我们提出了一种强化学习方法，可以有效地应用于提取器以协调训练和测试之间的优化。实验结果表明，我们的框架在模型大小相当的情况下明显优于强大的基线，并在 Multi-News、Multi-XScience 和 WikiCatSum 语料库上取得了最佳结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于引用灵活提取与信用感知的多文档摘要框架，通过分层提取和抽象机制解决长文本输入的挑战。
AI.motivation: 多文档摘要面临输入过长导致的训练困难，传统伪提取方法存在信息不足和训练测试目标不一致的问题。
AI.method: 构建分层提取器与抽象器，引入损失加权机制区分句子重要性，并利用强化学习优化提取器训练过程。
AI.result: 在Multi-News、Multi-XScience和WikiCatSum数据集上显著优于现有基线模型。
AI.conclusion: 信用感知机制与强化学习有效协调了训练与测试目标，提升了多文档摘要性能。

authors_text: Soravit Changpinyo,Doron Kukliansky,Idan Szpektor,Xi Chen,Nan Ding,Radu Soricut
title: All You May Need for VQA are Image Captions
title_zh: VQA 可能需要的只是图像标题
comment: 2022 Annual Conference of the North American Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics (NAACL 2022)
comments: 2022 Annual Conference of the North American Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics (NAACL 2022)
abstract: Visual Question Answering (VQA) has benefited from increasingly sophisticated models, but has not enjoyed the same level of engagement in terms of data creation. In this paper, we propose a method that automatically derives VQA examples at volume, by leveraging the abundance of existing image-caption annotations combined with neural models for textual question generation. We show that the resulting data is of high-quality. VQA models trained on our data improve state-of-the-art zero-shot accuracy by double digits and achieve a level of robustness that lacks in the same model trained on human-annotated VQA data.
summary: Visual Question Answering (VQA) has benefited from increasingly sophisticated models, but has not enjoyed the same level of engagement in terms of data creation. In this paper, we propose a method that automatically derives VQA examples at volume, by leveraging the abundance of existing image-caption annotations combined with neural models for textual question generation. We show that the resulting data is of high-quality. VQA models trained on our data improve state-of-the-art zero-shot accuracy by double digits and achieve a level of robustness that lacks in the same model trained on human-annotated VQA data.
abstract_zh: 视觉问答 (VQA) 受益于日益复杂的模型，但在数据创建方面却没有达到同等水平。在本文中，我们提出了一种方法，通过利用丰富的现有图像标题注释与用于文本问题生成的神经模型相结合，自动批量导出 VQA 示例。我们表明所得数据是高质量的。在我们的数据上训练的 VQA 模型将最先进的零样本精度提高了两位数，并达到了在人工注释的 VQA 数据上训练的同一模型所缺乏的鲁棒性水平。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于图像字幕自动生成高质量视觉问答（VQA）数据的方法。
AI.motivation: VQA领域模型发展迅速但数据创建投入不足，需自动化方法生成高质量训练数据。
AI.method: 结合现有图像字幕注释与神经文本问题生成模型，大规模合成VQA样本。
AI.result: 训练数据使模型零样本准确率提升超10%，且模型稳健性优于人工标注数据训练的模型。
AI.conclusion: 自动化生成的高质量VQA数据可有效提升模型性能，减少对人工标注数据的依赖。

ai_content.tldr: 提出一种基于图像字幕自动生成高质量视觉问答（VQA）数据的方法。
ai_content.motivation: VQA领域模型发展迅速但数据创建投入不足，需自动化方法生成高质量训练数据。
ai_content.method: 结合现有图像字幕注释与神经文本问题生成模型，大规模合成VQA样本。
ai_content.result: 训练数据使模型零样本准确率提升超10%，且模型稳健性优于人工标注数据训练的模型。
ai_content.conclusion: 自动化生成的高质量VQA数据可有效提升模型性能，减少对人工标注数据的依赖。

search_text: All You May Need for VQA are Image Captions VQA 可能需要的只是图像标题 Visual Question Answering (VQA) has benefited from increasingly sophisticated models, but has not enjoyed the same level of engagement in terms of data creation. In this paper, we propose a method that automatically derives VQA examples at volume, by leveraging the abundance of existing image-caption annotations combined with neural models for textual question generation. We show that the resulting data is of high-quality. VQA models trained on our data improve state-of-the-art zero-shot accuracy by double digits and achieve a level of robustness that lacks in the same model trained on human-annotated VQA data. 视觉问答 (VQA) 受益于日益复杂的模型，但在数据创建方面却没有达到同等水平。在本文中，我们提出了一种方法，通过利用丰富的现有图像标题注释与用于文本问题生成的神经模型相结合，自动批量导出 VQA 示例。我们表明所得数据是高质量的。在我们的数据上训练的 VQA 模型将最先进的零样本精度提高了两位数，并达到了在人工注释的 VQA 数据上训练的同一模型所缺乏的鲁棒性水平。 提出一种基于图像字幕自动生成高质量视觉问答（VQA）数据的方法。 Soravit Changpinyo,Doron Kukliansky,Idan Szpektor,Xi Chen,Nan Ding,Radu Soricut cs.CV,cs.CL
